title: Showing each decision's number
question: Should the viewer show each decision's id number (like d38), and where?
rationale: Show a faint id (d38) in the open decision's header and in the corner of every card and list row. Chosen because you refer to decisions by number, but until now the id only appeared in the page address — invisible while browsing. Kept faint and secondary so it never competes with the plain-language title that non-technical readers scan by (d16). In the compact list view the id sits at the front of each row.

history.from: In list view the id sat in the right-hand cluster, next to the category and date
history.reason: Moved the id to the front of each list row (right after the colour dot, before the title) with a fixed width, so the numbers line up in a column and are easy to scan down; card view keeps its id by the phase tag

options.label: A faint id in the detail header and on every card and row

tradeoffs.note: you can read a decision's number straight from the list or the open panel, without checking the address bar
adds a technical-looking 'd38' label, but kept faint and small so the title still leads (d16)
one more tiny element on each card; placed next to the phase tag so it reads as metadata
the list stays exactly as clean as before
you'd have to open each decision to learn its number

options.label: Don't show it — the id stays in the URL only

tradeoffs.note: the only way to see a number is to open a decision and read the page address
